title: The Queer Games Avant-Garde
creator: Bo Ruberg
description: A study built around interviews with queer independent game makers. It documents their works, design practices, communities, aesthetics, and resistance to narrow ideas about both games and representation.
useful: First-person accounts connect criticism to the conditions and intentions of making work outside dominant industry models.
audience: Developers, artists, critics, and researchers interested in queer games and alternative production cultures.
learning: Recognize a wider field of queer game-making practices
Connect aesthetics with community and production conditions
Use interviews as evidence without flattening different creators
